REFERENCE: This reference has multiple red flags indicating it is likely fabricated or contains significant errors: (1) The authors do not match any known IL-23 psoriatic arthritis phase 3 trial investigators; (2) The journal abbreviation is incorrect for NEJM; (3) The DOI is clearly invalid/fabricated; (4) NEJM rarely publishes active-comparator trials in PsA biologics; (5) The title lacks specificity (no drug name) which is atypical for major journal publications. Additionally, the format and character distribution suggest possible AI-generated or manually constructed fake citation. This reference should be considered inauthentic.
authors: The names Kearns, T. & Mahon, P. are plausible author names, but they do not match the known authors of any landmark IL-23 pathway trial in psoriatic arthritis. The seminal phase 3 trial targeting IL-23 in PsA with an active comparator was likely conducted by different authors. There is no obvious reason these specific authors would have authored this exact paper.
title: The title describes a phase 3, randomized, double-blind, active-comparator trial targeting IL-23 in psoriatic arthritis. This is a plausible and reasonable title structure for such a study. However, it is quite generic and doesn't specify the drug name (e.g., guselkumab, risankizumab, tildrakizumab), which is unusual for an actual published trial title.
journal_name: The New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM) is a prestigious, real medical journal. However, NEJM articles typically have shorter author lists and the journal is abbreviated as 'N Engl J Med', not 'The New England journal of medicine'. Also, NEJM rarely publishes active-comparator trials for psoriatic arthritis biologics — these typically appear in journals like Lancet, Annals of Rheumatic Diseases, or Arthritis & Rheumatology.
journal_volume: Volume 385, Issue 2 corresponds to NEJM's July 2021 publication. The volume number is plausible for NEJM.
journal_issue: Issue 2 is plausible for a weekly journal like NEJM.
journal_page: Pages 107-118 is a plausible page range for a NEJM article.
elocator: NEJM uses DOI format like '10.1056/NEJMoaXXXXXXX'. The provided DOI '10.3102.82/2t49dv3a-/rkgb.-hz263ubi' is not a valid DOI format. DOIs are structured as 10.<registrant>/<suffix> with the registrant being a number (e.g., 1056 for NEJM) and the suffix being alphanumeric. This DOI contains slashes, periods, and dashes in an incorrect pattern that doesn't match DOI standards.
publication_date: No explicit publication date is provided beyond the volume/issue information. Volume 385 Issue 2 would correspond to July 2021 for NEJM.
doi: The DOI '10.3102.82/2t49dv3a-/rkgb.-hz263ubi' is clearly fabricated/invalid. It does not conform to DOI syntax (10.<registrant>/<suffix>), contains unusual characters and structure, and does not resolve to any known publication. Real NEJM DOIs begin with '10.1056/NEJMoa'.
